_notes: Source registry for real-data drafts. These entries are not imported by the public app. Phase 15D-2 keeps all data in draft status and records manual normalization of flags and localized names.

sources.notes: Used for the official list of 48 teams and FIFA-listed group assignments in teams.real.draft.json.
Used to verify all 48 qualified teams and confederation grouping. No model memory used.
Flags were changed from nonexistent SVG paths to emoji strings to keep Team.flag as string without creating assets or pointing to missing files. This is a draft representation and not promoted.
Portuguese and Spanish names were localized in draft using common forms. These localized labels require later QA before any public promotion; FIFA English names remain the source basis for name.en.
groups.real.draft.json was generated from the existing group values in teams.real.draft.json. The original group assignments are covered by fifa-teams-2026; no new external data was collected.
Article/API has 104 match-centre links but lacks structured kickoff time, timezone, datetime_utc, city, and country for a safe Match draft. matches.real.draft.json was not created.

Phase 15F-2 PDF audit completed. The official PDF visually shows the full match schedule with match numbers 1-104, dates, kickoff times, ET timezone note, host-city rows, phase bands, group/team cells, and knockout labels. It is not marked ready-for-extraction or ready-for-production because the PDF text layer is not table-safe; stadium/city/country require separate line-by-line validation before extraction, and ET-to-UTC conversion requires a dedicated audit before any matches draft.

Used in Phase 15F-3 to document that US daylight saving time in 2026 is in effect from March 8 at 2 a.m. local time to November 1 at 2 a.m. local time. Supports validating that FIFA World Cup 2026 June/July ET times fall inside the DST period before UTC conversion.

Used in Phase 15F-3 to justify America/New_York as the technical IANA timezone for interpreting FIFA PDF times declared as Eastern Time (ET). Future conversion must use timezone-aware runtime/library behavior backed by IANA data or an equivalent audited method.
